zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley是Hugh Calveley和Margaret Marjory Done的兒子，於大約1440年出生在英國爾麗婭 白 巴福德。他於1455年與Christiana Cottingham在英國英格蘭爾麗婭 白 巴福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1515年在英國爾卡爾維勒享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: Calveley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Calverley
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Calverley in Yorkshire, named with Old English calfra (genitive plural of calf ‘calf’) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Alternatively, in some instances the name may be a variant of the habitational name Calveley, from a place so called in Cheshire (with the same meaning as above), or from Calverleigh in Devon, meaning ‘the clearing of the bare wood’ (Old English calu ‘bare, bald’ + wudu ‘wood’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley was born about 1440, in Lea by Backford, Cheshire, England as the son of Hugh Calveley and Margaret Marjory Done. He married Christiana Cottingham in 1455, in Lea by Backford, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 9 daughters. He died in 1515, in Calveley, Cheshire, England, at the age of 76.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley(은)는 대략 1440년 영국 체실르 리아 비 바ck퍼드에서 Hugh Calveley(와)과 Margaret Marjory Done의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1455년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 리아 비 바ck퍼드에서 Christiana Cottingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1515년 영국 체실르 캘벨리에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley родился около 1440 г. в местности леа би баккфорд, чешир, Англия. Его родителей звали Hugh Calveley и Margaret Marjory Done. Он женился на женщине по имени Christiana Cottingham в 1455 г. в местности леа би баккфорд, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 9 дочерей. Он умер в 1515 г. в местности калвели, чешир, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.
